module.name: client
module.purpose: Client crate for the user-facing session. event.rs defines ClientEvent, the user-facing event surface; session.rs implements ClientSession, a self-connecting client that mirrors HostSession's worker + shutdown model. new(name) stores the session name in a module-level SOURCE_NAME OnceLock, builds the user ClientEvent channel via build_channel(), initializes agent_tx as an empty SharedNetSender<SessionMessage> (Arc<Mutex<None>>), creates a fresh shutdown Arc<AtomicBool>, and starts the worker via spawn_worker. is_connected() reports whether the NetSender to the agent has been established. create_task now builds its task command with TaskSignature::new(request.clone()), so a fresh TaskId is allocated for new tasks, and sends SessionEvent::Task(signature, TaskEvent::Create { request }) through send_to_agent. cancel_task intentionally keeps a TaskSignature struct literal so it preserves the existing TaskId supplied by the caller instead of generating a new one. package_message stamps SOURCE_NAME, send_to_agent forwards through the shared agent_tx when connected, to_client_event maps content-bearing inbound task events to ClientEvent::Common(String), receiver() borrows the user event receiver, and close() signals shutdown and joins the worker. main.rs is the marix-client binary that loads Config, waits for connection readiness, sends one-shot or interactive prompts, drains events, and closes on exit.
